notes: Last king of Rome, known for his tyranny and exile, traditionally dated to the late 6th century BCE.

docs.title: Roman History, Book Book 2
docs.author: Cassius Dio
docs.excerpt: … who died in the time of Tarquin the Proud, leaving behind three or nine of her prophetic books. Of these the Romans bought either one or three, since her servant had destroyed the rest by fire because they would not give her as much gold as he asked.

docs.title: Letter LXXXII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: 18. 1 Kings xii. 10. Tarquin the Proud the last king of Rome was driven into exile because of his many acts of tyranny. Nu. xii. 3. Exod. xvii. 4. Exod. xxxii. 31, 32. Joh. x. 11, R.V.; Luke xv. 4, 5. Eph. vi. 4.

docs.title: Life of Augustus
docs.author: Suetonius
docs.excerpt: … the expulsion of Tarquin the Proud, to supply the place of those whom that king had slain, were called Conscripti, i. e. persons written or enrolled among the old senators, who alone were properly styled Patres.
